sections.title: Submarine Warfare — Can You Hide?
sections.description: The core promise: idle ships invisible, belt miners safe, loud actions obvious.

checks.goal: Idle ships are undetectable (even after 48hr DSC observation)
checks.detail: Idle emission = 0. DSC 48hr confidence = 4.7%.

checks.goal: Belt miner is safe for ~6hr even vs DSC (masking=3.0)
checks.detail: Belt miner (masking=3): 6hr=21.8%, 24hr=61%.

checks.goal: Open-space miner detectable by DSC after ~6hr integration
checks.detail: Open miner, DSC 6hr = 95%.

checks.goal: PNP scan detectable within ~30 min (VRS passive)
checks.detail: PNP emission=5, VRS: 1-sample=37.8%, 30min=95%. Cap=95%.

checks.goal: Shield regen barely detectable (even DSC, 12hr, open space)
checks.detail: Shield regen emission=0.2, DSC 12hr = 25.9%.

sections.title: Sensor Differentiation — Do They Feel Different?
sections.description: ACU = loud hunter, DSC = patient listener, VRS = generalist. Each should create a distinct playstyle.

checks.goal: DSC > VRS > ACU for passive detection of continuous signals
checks.detail: 2hr mining passive: DSC=72.7%, VRS=36.6%, ACU=12.2%.

checks.goal: ACU is genuinely poor at passive detection (< 20% for miner at 6hr)
checks.detail: ACU 6hr passive miner = 22.8%. ACU should need active scanning.

checks.goal: Matched filter specialization: ACU→pulse, DSC→continuous
checks.detail: ACU pulse=1.5 cont=0.7. DSC pulse=0.7 cont=1.25.

checks.goal: VRS is generalist: moderate gains, never best or worst
checks.detail: VRS pulse=1, continuous=1. Should be 1.0 (neutral).

sections.title: Drive Envelope Personalities
sections.description: Each drive class should have a distinctive EM signature. Military = brutal/fast, civilian = gentle/slow, industrial = hot/heavy.

checks.goal: Peak power ranking: military > industrial > civilian
checks.detail: DR-705 (military): peak=2.5. DR-505 (industrial): peak=1.5. DR-305 (civilian): peak=1.2

checks.goal: Military spool at peak detectable within a few samples (DSC)
checks.detail: DR-705 peak: power=10, DSC 1-sample=82.5%, 3-samples (15min)=95%.

checks.goal: Civilian drive still stealthy at 50% spool
checks.detail: DR-305 at 50% spool (90s): power=1.6971, confidence=9.7%.

checks.goal: Military sustain is quieter than civilian (stealthy at cruise)
checks.detail: DR-705 sustain=0.8, DR-305 sustain=1.

checks.goal: DR-305 (civilian): spool and cooldown durations are gameplay-meaningful
checks.detail: Spool=180s (3min), cooldown=120s (2min). Should be 1-5 min for catchable detection windows.

checks.goal: DR-505 (industrial): spool and cooldown durations are gameplay-meaningful
checks.detail: Spool=240s (4min), cooldown=180s (3min). Should be 1-5 min for catchable detection windows.

checks.goal: DR-705 (military): spool and cooldown durations are gameplay-meaningful
checks.detail: Spool=120s (2min), cooldown=150s (2.5min). Should be 1-5 min for catchable detection windows.

sections.title: Signature Analysis — What Can You Learn?
sections.description: Passive observation should reveal information: emission power estimation, activity classification, drive identification.

checks.goal: Round-trip: PNP scan (power=5)
checks.detail: At passive cap (95%). Estimated=3.7715 (underestimates true power=5). Use active scan for precise readings.

checks.goal: Round-trip: Mining (power=1)
checks.detail: At passive cap (95%). Estimated=0.8622 (underestimates true power=1). Use active scan for precise readings.

checks.goal: Round-trip: Belt miner (power=1)
checks.detail: Confidence=56.2%, estimated=1, error=0, top_match=mining.

checks.goal: Round-trip: Drive spool (power=4)
checks.detail: At passive cap (95%). Estimated=2.6668 (underestimates true power=4). Use active scan for precise readings.

checks.goal: All 3 drive classes identifiable by peak spool signature
checks.detail: DR-305 (civilian): peak=4.8, conf=30.7%, identified=YES. DR-505 (industrial): peak=6, conf=43.3%, identified=YES. DR-705 (military): peak=10, conf=82.5%, identified=YES

sections.title: Information Hierarchy — Escalating Detail at Escalating Cost
sections.description: From docs/dev/dsp.md: "Integration refines classification over time." Patient observation → awareness → identification.

checks.goal: Progressive revelation: 30m → anomaly, 2hr → type, 4hr → cap (95%)
checks.detail: Open miner vs DSC: 30m=26.7%, 2hr=72.7%, 6hr=95%, 12hr=95%. Passive cap=95%.

checks.goal: Classification improves with integration time
checks.detail: 2hr: top=mining (sep=0). 6hr: top=planet_scan (sep=0.0755). Wider separation = more confident classification.

checks.goal: Information cost hierarchy: passive=0, survey=moderate, PNP=loud
checks.detail: Passive emission=0. Survey emission=2. PNP emission=5. Each level reveals more but costs more signal.

sections.title: Information Tiers — What Do You Learn?
sections.description: Confidence maps to information quality: anomaly → class → type → analysis. Equipment and experience shift thresholds.

checks.goal: PNP scan progresses: anomaly immediately, analysis within 30min (VRS)
checks.detail: PNP VRS: 1-sample=37.8% [anomaly], 30min=95% [analysis].

checks.goal: Miner detection progresses through tiers over hours (DSC)
checks.detail: 1hr=45.4%[class], 2hr=72.7%[type], 4hr=93.3%[analysis], 6hr=95%[analysis], 8hr=95%[analysis], 12hr=95%[analysis]

checks.goal: Military drive spool at peak reaches type tier (DSC, 1 sample)
checks.detail: DR-705 peak DSC 1-sample: confidence=82.5%, tier=type.

checks.goal: Correlator module (bonus=0.05) shifts a marginal reading up one tier
checks.detail: Confidence=62%: default=class, with correlator=type. Thresholds shifted by 0.05.

sections.title: Envelope vs Sample Period — Scan Frequency Tradeoffs
sections.description: Spool durations are minutes. Passive samples every 1-10 minutes. Faster scanning catches more transients.

checks.goal: Spool-to-sample ratio: are spools catchable?
checks.detail: Sample period=300s (5min). Spool durations: DR-305 (civilian)=180s (3min), DR-505 (industrial)=240s (4min), DR-705 (military)=120s (2min). Spool/sample ratio: 40%-80%.

checks.goal: DR-305 (civilian): peak vs time-averaged detection
checks.detail: Envelope avg power=1.8707, duty cycle=100.3%, diluted power=1.8769. Peak conf=30.7%, time-avg conf=10.5%.

checks.goal: DR-505 (industrial): peak vs time-averaged detection
checks.detail: Envelope avg power=2.5019, duty cycle=140.3%, diluted power=3.511. Peak conf=43.3%, time-avg conf=19.7%.

checks.goal: DR-705 (military): peak vs time-averaged detection
checks.detail: Envelope avg power=5.447, duty cycle=90.3%, diluted power=4.9205. Peak conf=82.5%, time-avg conf=31.8%.

checks.goal: What sample period catches a military spool at peak?
Design question: how does the engine observe transient spool events?
checks.detail: Spools are now 2-4 minutes. With 5-min default sampling, a military spool occupies 40% of a sample window. A 1-min scanner catches every spool. A 10-min scanner has a 20% chance. Scan frequency is now a meaningful tactical choice.

sections.title: Self-Interference — Cooldown Degrades Scanning
sections.description: From dsp.md: own-ship emissions add to the noise the sensor must filter. Military drives arrive hot.

checks.goal: DR-305 (civilian): cooldown self-interference on scanning
checks.detail: Self-emission during cooldown degrades own sensor. DR-305 (civilian) cooldown peak=1.2000000000000002.

checks.goal: DR-505 (industrial): cooldown self-interference on scanning
checks.detail: Self-emission during cooldown degrades own sensor. DR-505 (industrial) cooldown peak=1.5.

checks.goal: DR-705 (military): cooldown self-interference on scanning
checks.detail: Self-emission during cooldown degrades own sensor. DR-705 (military) cooldown peak=2.7.

checks.goal: Military cooldown meaningfully degrades scanning (wait vs scan now)
checks.detail: DSC detecting miner 2hr: clean=72.7%, during DR-705 cooldown peak=31%. Self-emission=2.7.

checks.goal: Civilian cooldown has less scan impact than military
checks.detail: DSC detecting miner 2hr: clean=72.7%, DR-305 cooldown=48.2%, DR-705 cooldown=31%. Civilian degrades less than military.

sections.title: Gameplay Scenarios — Does the Timing Work?
sections.description: Real gameplay situations stress-tested against the timestamp model. Wolves, miners, wolf packs, busy systems.

checks.goal: Miner detects wolf PNP scan (builds confidence over samples)
checks.detail: Wolf PNP (power=5). VRS: 1 sample=37.8%, 3 samples (15min)=79.1%, 6 samples (30min)=95%. Awareness builds, not instant.

checks.goal: Wolf pack PNP raises noise floor, partially masking other signals
checks.detail: DSC finding miner (6hr): clean=95%, 1 wolf PNP=44%, 3 wolf PNP=25.3%. Wolf pack noise helps hide the miner.

checks.goal: Wolf pack is LOUDER than solo wolf (RMS scaling)
checks.detail: 3-wolf PNP signal (RMS)=8.6603 vs solo=5. 1 sample=79.1%, 15min=95%. Pack can't sneak for long.

checks.goal: DR-305 (civilian): spool warning window for flee decision
checks.detail: Spool lasts 180s (3min). Sample period=300s (5min). Catch probability: 60%.

checks.goal: DR-505 (industrial): spool warning window for flee decision
checks.detail: Spool lasts 240s (4min). Sample period=300s (5min). Catch probability: 80%.

checks.goal: DR-705 (military): spool warning window for flee decision
checks.detail: Spool lasts 120s (2min). Sample period=300s (5min). Catch probability: 40%.

checks.goal: Busy system (10 miners + 2 wolves + ECM): detection is degraded but works
checks.detail: Noise floor=6.8238 (baseline=1). Miner detection (DSC 6hr): 12%. Wolf PNP (VRS 1 sample): 6%, 30min: 8.5%.

checks.goal: Two ships, independent envelope phases — noise floor computed at shared timestamp
checks.detail: DR-705 at 5s (spool): emission=2.0412. DR-305 at 40s (spool): emission=0.5028. Combined noise floor=1.6307.

sections.title: Implementation Coverage — Formula Layer vs Design Docs
sections.description: What's built, what's designed but not yet implemented.

checks.goal: emission.ts — Source signal power
checks.detail: Implemented and tested.

checks.goal: noise.ts — Noise floor (stellar + ships + belt + ECM + shipNoiseFactor)
checks.detail: Implemented and tested.

checks.goal: snr.ts — SNR, masked SNR, integration gain
checks.detail: Implemented and tested.

checks.goal: detection.ts — Detection sigmoid, cumulative detection, matched filter
checks.detail: Implemented and tested.

checks.goal: passive.ts — Full passive pipeline, multi-source report
checks.detail: Implemented and tested.

checks.goal: envelope.ts — Drive ADSR envelope (spool/sustain/cooldown)
checks.detail: Implemented and tested.

checks.goal: signature.ts — Inverse sigmoid, power estimation, classification
checks.detail: Implemented and tested.

checks.goal: classification.ts — Information tiers, adjusted thresholds (equipment/experience)
checks.detail: Implemented and tested.

checks.goal: Active scan sweep model (envelopes.md)
checks.detail: Sensor LFO sweep pattern, checkpoint-based detection with cumulative probability per sweep

checks.goal: Self-interference (dsp.md)
checks.detail: Own-ship emissions degrade own sensor SNR (drive cooldown degrades scanning)

checks.goal: ECM/ECCM (scanning.md)
checks.detail: Intentional noise injection and counter-measures

checks.goal: Signal processor addon (scanning.md)
checks.detail: Correlator/amplifier/filter equipment modifying the signal chain

checks.goal: Filter tuning (dsp.md)
checks.detail: Bandwidth, noise rejection, spectral focus as tunable parameters

checks.goal: Component drift (envelopes.md)
checks.detail: Usage-based template sharpening (veteran sensors detect familiar signals better)

checks.goal: Chirp pattern fingerprinting (dsp.md)
checks.detail: Drive frequency sweep rate as a unique identifier per drive model

checks.goal: Distance/range within system (scanning.md)
checks.detail: Optional: spatial attenuation within a system abstraction

checks.goal: Information decay (scanning.md)
checks.detail: Stale contacts lose confidence over time

sections.title: Tuning Health — Are the Numbers Right?
sections.description: Validates that the constants produce reasonable gameplay characteristics.

checks.goal: Emission ranking: weapons > PNP > drive_spool (loudest = most aggressive actions)
checks.detail: Top 3: pvp_scan=8, weapons_fire=6, pnp_scan=5. Bottom: planet_scan=0.8, shield_regen=0.2, idle=0.

checks.goal: Integration gain shows diminishing returns (√N scaling)
Sigmoid transition zone is gradual (no brick walls)
checks.detail: 10%=1.6056, 50%=6, 90%=10.3944, width=8.7889. Steepness=0.5. Target: 4-12 width (very gradual).
